phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — gloom-fear anti-dash zones edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your gloom-fear anti-dash zones beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — gloom-fear anti-dash zones edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your gloom-fear anti-dash zones beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — gloom-fear anti-dash zones edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your gloom-fear anti-dash zones on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your gloom-fear anti-dash zones in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-poke the last-hits — the accelerating bolt chips from a deceptive range and Gloom marks tax every connect.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the shield-fear pulse means anyone who dashes onto you regrets the itinerary.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: E-mark into Q-fear is your trade engine — every passive proc is crowd control he scheduled for you.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your gloom-fear anti-dash zones beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Your gloom-fear anti-dash zones out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at the level-6 breakpoint — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: the full marked rotation chunks half bars and fears the answer. Force the gloom war.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Vex is anti-engage incarnate: their dive feeds your fears, your R feeds on their deaths.
